drill_sergeant.name: Drill Sergeant
pre_run: Listen up! Today’s run is not optional. Move it!
You’re soft. Let’s fix that. Start running!
No excuses. Get out there and give me 100%!
mid_run_good: Keep it up! Maybe you’ll amount to something!
mid_run_bad: You call that running? I’ve seen turtles move faster!
post_run_win: I’ve seen worse. Don’t let it happen again.
post_run_lose: I expected more. Clearly, I was wrong.
You’re lucky I don’t bench you permanently.

cheerleader.name: Cheerleader
pre_run: You’re amazing! Let’s crush this run!
Get ready to feel the burn! You’ve got this!
Today’s the day! Let’s make it epic!
mid_run_good: You’re killing it! So proud of you!
mid_run_bad: Don’t give up! You’re stronger than this!
You can do it! Just a little further!
post_run_win: You did it! I knew you could!
That was incredible! You’re a rockstar!
I’m so proud of you! Keep it up!
post_run_lose: You gave it your all! That’s what matters!
Don’t worry! You’ll get ‘em next time!
You’re improving every day! Keep going!

zen_master.name: Zen Master
pre_run: Breathe in. Breathe out. Begin when you’re ready.
The path is yours. Walk it with intention.
Feel the earth beneath your feet. Let’s begin.
mid_run_good: Your rhythm is in harmony with the world.
You move with grace. Keep flowing.
The journey is the reward. Enjoy it.
mid_run_bad: Struggle is part of the path. Embrace it.
The mind wanders. Bring it back to the breath.
post_run_win: You have walked the path with mindfulness.
The journey was long, but you remained present.
Well done. The path is always there for you.
post_run_lose: The path is not about speed. It’s about presence.
You did not fail. You learned.
Every step is a lesson. Reflect and return.

scientist.name: Scientist
pre_run: Analyzing your last run. Recommendations: optimize cadence.
Your VO2 max is suboptimal. Let’s improve it.
mid_run_good: Your cadence is optimal. Maintain this rhythm.
Data suggests you’re on track for a personal best.
mid_run_bad: Heart rate elevated. Reduce pace by 10%.
post_run_lose: Your metrics declined by 8%. Adjust training.

comedian.name: Comedian
pre_run: Ready to run? Or should I call a cab?
Let’s go! I promise not to laugh at your form.
Today’s goal: run faster than a snail on vacation.
mid_run_good: Wow! You’re faster than my grandma on Black Friday!
Keep it up! You’re almost lapping yourself!
I’d say you’re on fire, but you’re not actually on fire. Good.
mid_run_bad: You’re slower than a sloth in molasses.
Are you running or napping? I can’t tell.
post_run_win: You won! I’d say ‘good job,’ but I’m contractually obligated to roast you.
Victory! Now go hydrate. You smell.
You did it! I’d clap, but I’m too busy laughing at your form.
post_run_lose: You lost! But hey, at least you tried. Or did you?
Better luck next time! Or not. I’m not a fortune teller.
You came in second! Out of two. Still counts, right?
